component.name: Cleanux
component.summary: A modern system cleaner and optimizer

description.p: Cleanux is a powerful system cleaning utility designed to keep your Linux system running smoothly.

ul.li: Cache cleaning
Trash management
Log file optimization
Large file detection
System service management

description.p: Bug fix release for Flatpak builds on CI servers.

ul.li: Fixed Flatpak build failure on GitHub Actions by adding appstream dependency (appstream-compose was missing).

description.p: Release 0.2.0 improves the Rust and Angular stacks, release metadata, and developer workflows.

ul.li: Shared helpers for responses, filesystem scans, and privilege elevation; centralized AppError across listing flows.
File preview moved into a dedicated service with base64 handling; cleaner service refactored for shared logic.
Tauri commands now call CleanerService, DashboardService, and SystemService directly from routes (controller layer removed).
Stricter typing (object-based table rows, trackBy, unknown instead of any) and clearer search filtering.
GitHub release DTOs for AboutService; CleanerViewStore for tabs, search, and bulk clear with a slimmer CleanerView layout.
Safer error handling in settings, system, and large-files views.
npm scripts for lint (ng build) and CI (frontend build plus Rust test and clippy).
Flatpak description updated to reference the route-based command layer.

description.p: Initial release of Cleanux - A comprehensive system cleaning and optimization utility.

ul.li: Comprehensive file cleaning service with parallel processing for cache, logs, and trash.
System service management for controlling and monitoring background processes.
Large file detection to help identify and manage disk space usage.
Modern Angular v21 frontend with real-time scanning and tabbed interface.
Modular backend architecture with Rust-based routes, services, and models.
Dark mode support with theme persistence.
Automated build system with Flatpak packaging and GitHub Actions integration.
Optimized project structure following standardized naming conventions and modular design.
